title: penstemon jamesii subsp. typicus
intro: Penstemon jamesii subsp. typicus, also known as the Rocky Mountain Penstemon, is a perennial plant species native to the Rocky Mountains in North America. It belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits like reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Herbal preparations of Penstemon jamesii subsp. typicus include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal and health benefits.
